name: California Primary — June 2, 2026 (statewide)
notes: Top-two primary — top two finishers regardless of party advance to Nov 3 general (except for nonpartisan county/judicial offices, which are decided in June if a candidate gets >50%). No statewide ballot measures on the June primary; the qualified statewide measures are on the Nov general.

races.notes: Polymarket primary-winner odds (auto-refreshed). Top two advance to Nov. ~60 candidates filed; toggle "show minor" to see all.

scandal.summary: His former hedge fund Farallon Capital invested in coal, fossil-fuel projects, and private prisons — repeatedly raised by rivals during the April 2026 debate, despite his climate-activism platform.
scandal.searchQuery: Tom Steyer Farallon coal private prisons fossil fuels

finance.summary: $132M self-funded; founded Farallon Capital hedge fund

culturalLean.rationale: Climate-progressive billionaire; econ-focused, less identity-politics-coded.
